iterations.strategy: Root cause: the iter_0 plan allocated 64 thread-local hash maps of 4MB each (256MB total anonymous mmap), triggering ~65,536 page faults during parallel initialization — this unaccounted overhead consumed ~80ms of the 90ms total, while actual scan work was only 5ms. Additionally, the two-pass design forced two sequential scans of the same ~100K-row block. Fix (Category A+D): collapse to a single-threaded single-pass design. One scan over the ~1 valid block simultaneously builds a 1MB global MAX map (65536 slots) keyed on (adsh_code, tagver_code) and collects candidates — both restricted to fy[adsh_code]==2022 rows, which is semantically equivalent to the full subquery because n.adsh=m.adsh pins each outer row to one adsh_code with fixed fy. Post-scan, candidates are post-filtered against the max map (expected ~500 pass), sorted, and top-100 decoded. Single-threaded eliminates all thread-init and merge overhead; 1MB map generates negligible page faults; one scan instead of two halves memory traffic.
